skill.name: backlink-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes backlink profiles, link quality, toxic link risks, competitor gaps, and outreach opportunities for SEO planning.
skill.summary: Audit backlink quality and find link building opportunities.
skill.author: aaron-he-zhu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found them in Markdown examples, SEO terminology, example URLs, and relative documentation links. No executable scripts, hidden network requests, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are Markdown code fences and output templates. They show user prompts and report formats, not executable shell commands or Ruby backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged ranges are fenced Markdown examples and plain text templates. No script file, command runner, or user-input command construction is present.

low_findings.title: Example URLs Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are examples in backlink and disavow documentation. They are not used by code to fetch, post, or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside explanatory Markdown and a sample disavow file. No network API, connector call, or automatic request logic surrounds them.

low_findings.title: Relative Reference Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts are relative Markdown links to connector documentation and related skills. They do not read files or access arbitrary paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines are documentation hyperlinks. There is no file-system API, path concatenation, or user-controlled file access.

low_findings.title: SEO Terms Misclassified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from SEO language, outreach text, and scoring tables. No cryptographic function or system probing is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language SEO guidance, email templates, or scoring rubric entries. Reviewed context showed no cryptographic code, host discovery, or reconnaissance workflow.

content.user_title: Audit Backlink Profiles and Find Link Opportunities
content.value_statement: Backlink reviews can be slow when data lives across exports, SEO tools, and competitor research. This skill structures link quality checks, toxic link review, gap analysis, and outreach planning.
actual_capabilities: Creates backlink profile summaries with referring domains, authority metrics, anchor text, and link velocity.
Scores link quality using authority, relevance, traffic, placement, anchor text, and follow status.
Flags toxic backlink patterns and suggests review or disavow actions.
Compares competitor link profiles to identify link gap opportunities.
Provides outreach templates for broken links, guest posts, resource pages, and unlinked mentions.
Maps backlink metrics into CITE-related citation and trust checks.
limitations: Requires user-provided exports or connected SEO data sources for real metrics.
Does not verify live backlinks without an external link database or SEO tool.
Disavow recommendations still need human review before upload.
Outreach templates must be personalized before sending.

use_cases.title: Audit an Existing Backlink Profile
use_cases.description: Review backlink exports, score link quality, and prioritize toxic links for manual review.

use_cases.title: Find Competitor Link Gaps
use_cases.description: Compare competitor referring domains and build a prioritized list of attainable link opportunities.

use_cases.title: Plan Outreach Campaigns
use_cases.description: Select outreach angles, adapt templates, and track expected effort for link acquisition campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Domain
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this backlink export for [domain]. Summarize profile health, top risks, strongest links, and immediate next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Toxic Links
prompt_templates.prompt: Check these backlinks for toxic patterns. Group links by risk level and explain which entries need manual review or disavow.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Competitors
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare our backlink profile against [competitor domains]. Identify shared competitor links we lack and rank opportunities by value.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Outreach Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backlink gap analysis to create an outreach plan with segments, templates, personalization notes, and success metrics.

output_examples.input: A backlink export for a SaaS domain with authority metrics and anchor text.
output_examples.output: A profile health summary with quality distribution, anchor text risks, strongest links, and recommended next actions.

output_examples.input: Competitor referring domain lists for three competing sites.
output_examples.output: A prioritized opportunity table showing domains that link to competitors, likely outreach angles, effort, and expected impact.

output_examples.input: A list of suspicious backlinks from low-quality sites.
output_examples.output: A toxic link review grouped into disavow, investigate, monitor, and ignore recommendations with short rationale.

best_practices: Provide backlink exports with source domain, target URL, anchor text, follow status, and authority metrics.
Review high-risk disavow recommendations manually before submitting anything to Google Search Console.
Personalize outreach with specific page references and clear value for the recipient.
anti_patterns: Do not treat low domain authority alone as proof that a link is toxic.
Do not send outreach templates without checking the recipient, page context, and publication guidelines.
Do not disavow broad domains when only one low-quality URL is the issue.

faq.question: Does this skill collect backlink data automatically?
faq.answer: Only when a compatible link database or SEO tool is connected. Otherwise, it uses data provided by the user.

faq.question: Can it create a disavow file?
faq.answer: It can guide disavow formatting and recommendations. A human should review every entry before upload.

faq.question: What data should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide source domains, target URLs, anchor text, follow status, authority metrics, and recent gains or losses.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple competitors?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare several competitor profiles and identify domains that link to them but not to you.

faq.question: Does it guarantee ranking improvements?
faq.answer: No. It supports backlink analysis and planning, but search rankings depend on many external factors.

faq.question: Is it useful without paid SEO tools?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide exports or manually collected data. Automated metrics will be limited without connected tools.
